content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 4, 1989




June E. Sanko
Deputy County Clerk-Recorder
County of Mendocino
Office of the Registrar of Voters
P.O. Box 148
Ukiah, CA 95482
                                          Re:  Our File No. G-89-194

Dear Ms. Sanko:

     We have received your letter requesting advice concerning the
county's duty to retain the statement of facts filed pursuant
to Government Code Section 53051.  We have forwarded your letter
to the Secretary of State's office.  The Secretary of State is
responsible for compiling the "Roster of Public Agencies" from the
information submitted pursuant to Section 53051.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Diane M. Griffiths
                                          General Counsel

                                          [signature]

                                          By:  Kathryn E. Donovan
                                               Counsel, Legal Division




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

MARSHA A. YOUNG
COUNTY CLERK-RECORDER

EX-OFFICIO CLERK
OF THE
SUPERIOR COURT

REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

TELEPHONE: AREA (707)
463-4379 - CLERK
463-4376 - RECORDER
463-4371 - ELECTIONS

COUNTY OF MENDOCINO
OFFICE OF THE REGISTRAR OF VOTERS
P.O. BOX 148
UKIAH, CALIFORNIA 95482

March 27, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Legal Division:

Please advise us of the length of time we need to retain
the Statement of Facts - Public Agencies - Pursuant to
California Government Code Section 53051.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

MARSHA A. YOUNG
County Clerk-Recorder

June E. Sanko
June E. Sanko
Deputy

. . - .

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 4, 1989

Anthony L. Miller
Chief Deputy Secretary of State
1230 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Miller:

     We are referring to you a letter from June E. Sanko, Deputy
County Clerk-Recorder of Mendocino County.  Ms. Sanko has
requested guidance concerning the county's duty to retain the
statement of facts filed with the Secretary of State in accordance
with Government Code Section 53051.

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By:  Kathryn E. Donovan
     Counsel, Legal Division


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from the California Fair Political Practices Commission dated April 4, 1989. It is addressed to June E. Sanko, Deputy County Clerk-Recorder of the County of Mendocino, Office of the Registrar of Voters, P.O. Box 148, Ukiah, CA 95482. The letter is in response to File No. G-89-194. The Commission states that they have received Ms. Sanko's letter requesting advice concerning the county's duty to retain the statement of facts it files pursuant to Government Code Section 53051. They have forwarded her letter to the Secretary of State's office, which is responsible for compiling the "Roster of Public Agencies" from the information submitted pursuant to Section 53051. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel [Signature] By: Kathryn E.
